model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With `train_max_steps=256`, the script is sample-limited more than epoch-limited. Increasing batch size lets each fixed step cover more CIFAR-10 examples, and a modest LR increase keeps Adam's early optimization progress from slowing after the larger-batch change. This is a simple, low-risk first move that targets faster loss reduction within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The cosine schedule degraded this short-budget run, so the next clean test is to remove that regression and push the fixed-step regime further toward sample efficiency. With 256 verifier steps, doubling batch size again should expose the model to roughly a full epoch of distinct examples while keeping the successful Adam setup otherwise intact.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the batch-size increase, the model now sees substantially more data within the fixed 256-step budget. The next clean test is to scale Adam's step size upward to match that larger batch regime and improve early optimization speed without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization side is now materially better, so the next simple lever is modestly increasing model capacity while staying in the same architecture family. A slightly wider convolutional trunk and a less restrictive classifier head should let the model use the larger-batch, higher-LR setup more effectively without introducing structural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last capacity increase paid off, which suggests the current bottleneck is still representational rather than optimization-only. Adding one more residual block keeps the architecture simple and in-family while giving the wider trunk more nonlinear refinement capacity within the same fixed training budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The architecture is now strong enough that the next useful lever is optimizer behavior rather than more capacity. In this short-budget regime, AdamW can regularize more cleanly than coupled Adam weight decay, and a slightly lower second-moment beta often helps the optimizer react faster over only 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"AdamW plus the faster second-moment setting regressed, so the better baseline is the prior Adam configuration. From there, a cleaner next test is slightly stronger regularization through weight decay, which may help the wider, deeper network generalize better after nearly a full-epoch worth of large-batch updates.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Stronger weight decay clearly hurt, so the safer baseline is the earlier regularization level. With the wider and slightly deeper network, the next useful check is whether the current `2e-3` step size is now a bit too aggressive; a modest LR reduction may improve final validation loss without undoing the gains from the larger-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            nn.Linear(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, FC_HIDDEN),","old":"            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The latest LR reduction hurt, so the stronger optimizer setting should be restored. The more interesting next architecture test is to finally use `CHANNEL_MULT` for a simple stage transition: downsample once, widen the feature map, and then do residual refinement at the higher channel count. That should improve receptive field and representational power without making the model deeper in a complicated way.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The downsample-and-widen stage was a large win, which suggests this revised architecture still benefits from additional channel capacity. A moderate width increase is the next simple test: it preserves the successful structure, keeps the optimization setup unchanged, and should improve representational power without introducing extra architectural machinery.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Widening the convolutional trunk further was too much, so the stronger stage-transition architecture should be kept at the earlier `BASE_CHANNELS = 12`. A cheaper place to add capacity is the classifier head: increasing `FC_HIDDEN` can improve class separation with much less optimization and compute disruption than another trunk-width increase.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The successful pattern so far is that well-placed capacity increases, not optimizer tweaks, are driving the gains. With the downsampled wider stage and larger classifier now working well, adding one more residual refinement block at the high-channel, lower-resolution stage is a natural next test with relatively contained extra compute.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The third residual block did not justify its extra complexity, so the best-performing stage-transition architecture should be restored. From there, the next strong fixed-step hypothesis is higher sample throughput: increasing batch size again may let the model exploit the verifier budget better now that the architecture and classifier are substantially stronger.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch moved away from the best regime, so the stronger architecture should go back to `BATCH_SIZE = 256`. From that baseline, a modest LR increase is the next clean optimization test: the model now has substantially more useful capacity than earlier versions, so it may benefit from a slightly more aggressive step size while staying in the same fast fixed-step training setup.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The LR increase moved away from the best regime, so the stronger architecture should return to `2e-3`. A useful next regularization test is mild label smoothing, which often improves CIFAR validation loss by softening overconfident predictions without requiring longer training or more architectural changes.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing did not help this budget, so the loss should return to plain cross-entropy. A better regularization test for the current high-capacity head and widened residual stage is very light dropout, which may reduce overconfidence with less optimization distortion than smoothing.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The light dropout regressed, so the stronger architecture should return to the no-dropout setting. The biggest recent win came from enlarging the classifier head, which suggests that capacity is still bottlenecked there more than in the convolutional trunk; increasing `FC_HIDDEN` again is a simple, targeted continuation of the most successful trend.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The second head expansion overreached, so the best-performing classifier size should be restored. A better last-stage capacity test is to increase the post-downsampling channel multiplier instead of the MLP head, since the biggest structural gain came from widening after spatial reduction and the resulting compute increase should remain manageable at that lower resolution.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher channel multiplier clearly overshot, so the final test should return to the strongest architecture shape. With that restored, the remaining clean lever is to slightly relax weight decay: stronger regularization already hurt, and the best model may still be mildly underfitting given the short fixed-step budget and the larger classifier head.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}
